items.text: Engine signal() branches on workflow residency: Resident delivers immediately, NonResident records and defers, Terminal returns typed error, Unknown returns WorkflowNotFound.
SignalResumeHandoff::deliver_deferred() is invoked when a workflow transitions from Suspended to Resident, delivering all queued signals in FIFO order.
A signal to a non-resident workflow appears in the workflow's durable history as a SignalReceived event even before the workflow resumes.
On engine restart, in-memory SignalResumeHandoff is empty; AD replay re-delivers recorded signals from history during deterministic replay (no double-delivery).
On SIGTERM or SIGINT, the server stops accepting new connections and new workflow starts within 100ms of signal receipt.
Connected workers receive a drain signal indicating no new tasks will be dispatched; workers finish in-flight tasks.
The server awaits completion of all in-flight activities up to the configured drain timeout before shutting down the engine.
Activities that exceed the drain timeout are surfaced as retryable failures via the existing LostWorkerReport path.
Clean drain exits with code 0; timeout-exceeded drain exits with code 1.
No event writes are lost during shutdown — the engine flushes all pending appends before the store connection closes.
GET /metrics returns Prometheus exposition format text with all registered metrics and their current values.
Workflow lifecycle metrics: aion_workflows_started_total and aion_workflows_completed_total counters with namespace and type/status labels.
Activity metrics: aion_activities_dispatched_total counter, aion_activities_completed_total counter (with outcome label), and aion_activity_duration_seconds histogram.
Infrastructure metrics: aion_connected_workers gauge, aion_inflight_activities gauge, aion_store_operation_duration_seconds histogram.
Signal and schedule metrics: aion_signals_delivered_total counter (with residency label), aion_schedules_fired_total counter.
GET /health/live returns 200 when the process is running and the beamr scheduler is responsive; 503 otherwise.
GET /health/ready returns 200 when the store is reachable and the runtime is initialized; 503 otherwise.
Both probes respond within 100ms under normal operation (no blocking store queries in the probe path).
All server output uses tracing with a JSON subscriber; no println, eprintln, or unstructured log macro output exists in production paths.
Engine operations emit spans with workflow_id and namespace fields; activity dispatch adds activity_type and worker_id.
Error events include the typed error variant name as a structured field, not just a Display string.
aion-server loads configuration from aion.toml with all sections (server, store, runtime, drain, auth, metrics, namespaces) parsed and validated at startup.
Environment variables with AION_ prefix override TOML values (e.g. AION_STORE_URL overrides store.url).
CLI flags override both environment and file values; precedence is CLI > env > file > defaults.
Invalid configuration (missing required fields, malformed URLs, conflicting settings) produces a clear error message naming the field and exits with code 2 before any server initialization.
The default configuration (no file, no env) starts a development server with an in-memory store on localhost — usable for local testing without any config.
When auth.enabled=true, requests without a valid Bearer token are rejected with HTTP 401 / gRPC UNAUTHENTICATED before reaching any handler.
The token's namespace claim scopes all operations; a token for namespace A cannot start, signal, query, cancel, or list workflows in namespace B.
Worker connections authenticate with the same token mechanism; the token's namespace determines which activity tasks the worker receives.
JWKS keys are cached with configurable refresh interval; a key rotation does not require server restart.
When auth.enabled=false (default), no authentication is required and the namespace is taken from request metadata (existing NamespaceGuard behaviour).
The auth module compiles only when cfg(feature='auth') is active; the base binary has no jsonwebtoken or JWKS dependencies.

The workflow gRPC service exposes a Reopen RPC with ReopenRequest (namespace, workflow id, optional run id) and a ReopenResponse that returns the reopened run id and its projected Running status, mirroring Cancel; the proto regenerates cleanly.

A dedicated INVALID_STATE wire error code carries the AD-012 reopen precondition failures (run not terminal, terminal for a non-Failed reason, or already Running) with error_type 'InvalidState' and the reason, distinct from NOT_FOUND and BACKEND; WorkflowNotFound still maps to NOT_FOUND.

The server exposes reopen over gRPC (WorkflowGrpcService::reopen) and HTTP (POST /workflows/reopen), both routed through one shared handler that resolves the run, calls Engine::reopen_workflow, and maps every EngineError to the right wire/HTTP code (INVALID_STATE -> gRPC FailedPrecondition / HTTP 409); the terminal-Failed precondition is the engine's, surfaced not re-implemented in the handler.

The client SDK exposes Client::reopen(workflow_id, optional run_id) returning the reopened run and its Running status, with transport and convert support mirroring cancel, and a distinct typed ClientError for the InvalidState case.

The CLI exposes `aion reopen <workflow-id> [--run-id]`, calling Client::reopen and printing the now-Running run, or the typed failure ('not reopenable: <reason>' for InvalidState, 'not found' for WorkflowNotFound) — never a raw transport error.

Every reopen error path — not found, not terminal, terminal for a non-Failed reason, already Running — is proven at the gRPC, HTTP, and CLI layers with the correct typed code and message, and the happy path returns Running.

Every file touched by the reopen surface stays within the 500-code-line limit; the pre-existing over-limit files (api/grpc.rs, client ops.rs, cli main.rs, api/handlers/workflows.rs) are split by responsibility as the reopen additions land.
